subject: Geography
title: The Partition of India
description: A quick win: understand The Partition of India.

hook.text: In 1947, a British lawyer who'd never been to India drew its new borders in five weeks.

buildup.text: Cyril Radcliffe divided India and Pakistan based on census data and outdated maps.

discovery.text: Villages learned which country they belonged to from radio broadcasts the next morning.

twist.text: The partition triggered the largest mass migration in history—15 million people displaced.

climax.text: Up to two million people died in the violence that followed the new borders.

punchline.text: One man, five weeks, and millions of shattered lives.

quiz.question: How long did Cyril Radcliffe have to draw the India-Pakistan border?
